---
title: "Lecture 4 — Population Regulation"
subtitle: "Density dependence and its consequences"
event: "ECOL 3500 — Ecology"
location: "Room 200, Biological Sciences"
date: today
format:
  drake-teach-coral-beamer:
    slide-level: 2
---

## Key ideas

- Population growth slows as density increases
- Feedback creates equilibrium behavior
- Parameters map to interpretable mechanisms

## Logistic model

$$
\frac{dN}{dt} = rN\left(1 - \frac{N}{K}\right)
$$

## Discussion prompt

What ecological processes could shift $K$ over a semester timescale?
